Session #2 Takeaways
• Clearing up any confusion or questions stemming from
session #1
• Diving deeper into the various mechanisms and tactics
needed to horizontally scale
• Better understanding of how wider audiences, multiple
objectives, effective landing pages, and other add-ons can
improve your results
• Step-by-step guide of how to build these additional
components into your account
• Examples of what I always look for to ensure an account is
pacing well and staying on track
• Dissecting the complex facets of bidding, budgeting, ad
placements, creative, and more

1. Feedback & Questions
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 4 of 93 --

Feedback & Questions
• Q: “Where do I start?”
• Q: “What if I’m not ecommerce?”
• A: Mostly starting with introducing new prospecting
audiences, as we discussed last time. Then rework your
remarketing with multiple pitches to different parts of a
lower funnel audience.
• Once you see stable success, coming after 7-14 days,
increase budgets vertically very slowly, ~20% every 48
hours.
• Follow the same methods for scaling on audiences, except
the lookalikes might not be created around ecommerce
events. Very similar tactics used, however.
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

Feedback & Questions
Q: “When do I stop a prospecting ad set and when do I
scale it?”
A: Generally speaking, I’m looking at breaking even over a
30 day period on prospecting. If I’m able to bring brand
new people into the funnel and recover my costs within a
month, then I’m doing something right.
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 9 of 93 --

Feedback & Questions
Q: We are all talking about scaling, but how do you
deescale (ex: easter holidays weekend in argentina have
really bad performance on some clientes), so i need to stop
or decrease budget, to put it up again on Monday) how do
you do this? I understand that you should do it verticaly
decreasing budget right?
A: Yes. Slowly decreasing budgets over time is okay to
“descale.”
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 10 of 93 --

Feedback & Questions
Q: Does the pixel need to be seasoned for each standard
event or custom conversion? How do you go about
optimal "seasoning" and how do you know when it is
done?
A: Seasoning the pixel has lots of parts, but mostly it means
driving more data into the events on your site to increase
traffic. As you spend more, results improve because
Facebook has more data.
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

3. More Prospecting
Recommendations
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 14 of 93 --

Diving Deeper into Audiences
• The audiences discussed in session #1 are generally the core
of what you’ll want set up in order to scale.
• However, there are additional audiences that are very
successful for scaling in the TOFU area.
• The best performing audiences generally speaking are
dynamic, meaning they are updated based on pixel actions.
Dynamic audiences and therefore their lookalikes will almost
always out perform static audiences, due to the nature of
regeneration.
• Remember: lookalike success depends on signals, so the
more data Facebook has, the better off your audience will be.
• If adds to cart aren’t a strong signal for conversion and the
add to cart to conversion percentage is low, then an add to
cart lookalike will not perform as well.
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 15 of 93 --

Prospecting Audiences for Scale
After interviewing multiple ad agencies and consultants, here are
some additional audiences that are popular for scaling.
• 1% 2X Purchasers
• 2%+ Purchasers
• 10% VIP audience + relevant interest
• Specific interests separated out by ad set. (Usually found in
Audience Insights)
• 1% Page View (not 2X) last 15 days
• 1% 30 or 60 day categorical WCA, meaning they look at a
specific product category
• Wide Audiences with almost no targeting
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 16 of 93 --

Wide Audience for Prospecting
Zero targeting,
optimizing for conversion
or value (in BETA).
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 17 of 93 --

Audience Insightswww.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 18 of 93 --

Broadmatch Dynamic Product Ads
• Broadmatch Dynamic Product Ads are essential to
scaling.
• They use your product catalog to populate images of
best-selling products to an audience Facebook
believes will convert the most.
• FB: With broad audience targeting, you can reach
people who've expressed interest in your products
(or products similar to yours), even if they haven't
visited your website or app yet. When you target a
broad audience, people in this audience
automatically see products from your catalog that
are likely relevant to them.
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 19 of 93 --

Broadmatch Dynamic Product Ads
• Use an incredibly wide audience of your best
converting segments, excluding 180 day visitors
and fans
• Ensure the offer is truly irresistible
• Ensure all your products are sized correctly on
your DPA
• Measure in a 30 day window (latency is big, but
incredibly worthwhile)
• Bid for 7 day click/1 day view (widest)
• Most successful with a well-seasoned pixel. The
more data the better it’ll be.
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 20 of 93 --

In ad creation flow, choose
objective “Catalog Sales”
under “Conversion”
objective.

4. Dynamic Product Ads
for Remarketing
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 23 of 93 --

Dynamic Product Ads
• As you drive more traffic into your funnel, you will greatly
benefit from having more complex Dynamic Product Ads
(DPAs) set up.
• The more complex the funneling with DPAs, the better
off you will be.
• This tactic is especially relevant to ecommerce
companies, but it’s also an important component for all
FB advertisers to employ.
• Commonly, Dynamic Product Ad Audiences will have
windows of 7, 14, 30 days.
• They get more complex the more data you have.
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 24 of 93 --

Dynamic Product Ads
The options are endless!
Many times, I will have two
DPA tests going in Phase
two, where I’ll be testing:
Viewed / Added To Cart
and Only Added to Cart.
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 25 of 93 --

Product Sets For Cross/Up Selling
• As you drive more users into your site, you have the
ability to use cross-selling and upselling to show
users ads for something they’ve bought in one
collection and either upsell them or sell them
another category that compliments that product.
• This only works if you have plenty of users and
they’re browsing multiple collections.
• It’s all about recapturing the demand with dynamic
ads, created for you, which works incredibly well.
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

go overboard.
• Especially when trying to hook new TOFU traffic, it
makes sense to be testing multiple landing pages.

6. Cranking Up Your Creative
and Copy
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 44 of 93 --

Creative
• When scaling, you need a TON of creative.
• Your should refresh your ad creative at least once per
week, depending on how fast you’re scaling.
• I never put more than two ads in an ad set and even
that’s stretching it.
• When you’re scaling, utilizing more than two or even
one, can push Facebook to make quick decisions,
pigeon-holing it into only showing one and wasting time
you spent building the other.
• Once launched, your creative should be at least a 6/10
on relevance score on top of funnel, to show decent
quality.
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 45 of 93 --

99% of Scaling Accounts...
Have link posts, photo posts, and video posts running.
These are the most popular creative vehicles for scale.
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 52 of 93 --

Copy
• Every six months we throw out all our assumptions and
test brand new ideas on ad copy.
• Shorter headline copy always wins.
• Try to stick to having no more than fifteen words.
• 8/10 times having a period at the end of a sentence
works better than exclamation points or question
marks.
• Use emojis!
• Action words continue to be our profit drivers.
• Mentioning company name doesn’t matter as much as
you might think.
• Price point is irrelevant if the ad copy is truly engaging.
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 53 of 93 --

Copy
• There are some ad copy best practices that continue working
no matter what.
• If there’s someone you’ve worked with, or a well-known media
outlet has given you an endorsement, definitely mention them!
• High ratings and external validation BOTH matter.
– “This changed my life and literally saved me.”
– Numbers always work like a charm.
– “Over 100k downloads to date”
– “Rated 5 Stars on iTunes”
– “5 Ways Product X Will Change Your Life”
– People always love FREE. (Who doesn’t?!)
– People also really love super simple promo codes. Think
about it: FB20 vs JY485-ECHO
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 54 of 93 --

Copy
• Give people a flexible way out
– “Try FREE for 30 days.”
– “Hassle-free returns.”
– “Cancel anytime.”
• Position yourself against your known competitor
– “The ten reasons we get hired over Joe’s Plumbing.”
• Have a clear CTA up-front.
– “Take our money. 20% Off NOW”
• Try asking questions
– “Annoyed with the wait?”
– “Sick of designer prices?”
• If made in USA, definitely mention it!
• Imply demand scarcity
– “Only 10 left!”
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

Copy

• [discount]

• [emoji]
• [endorsement]
• [number]
• [anything free]
• [implied scarcity]
• “Made in California & never had a return. Take 15% off
using code FB15.”
• “Our best-selling model is back-in-stock! Grab it before
it’s gone with our BOGO running now!

7. Recommended Placements
for Expansion
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 57 of 93 --

Placement Splits
• Most advertisers focus 80-90% of their spend on Mobile News
Feed, which is where Facebook’s most daily active users are.
• One of the best ways to control your costs is to split placements.
• There are varying schools of thought on this tactic, but at a
minimum splitting out Instagram from the rest of the pack is
important.
• It’s especially helpful in the lower parts of the funnel to split
placements out, because you can ensure the creative matches the
placement well (sized correctly and message is aligned) and it’ll
help you to achieve a better audience penetration.
• One of the most common ways people can lose money is by
setting the placement to default. In many cases, these defaults will
spend on Audience Network, which is currently a much lower
quality ad placement.
•
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 58 of 93 --

Placement Splitswww.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 59 of 93 --

Experimenting with
Placement Splits
• Try Splitting: Instagram out in the lower funnel. Ensures
Instagram gets the most impressions it can and creative
can be sized properly, along with ad copy.
• Try Splitting: Desktop News Feed out in very low funnel.
Helps to ensure those who are thinking about buying quite
a bit, convert on desktop, which they normally do.
• Try Splitting: Experimental placements out, such as
Audience Network. Not saying it’s inherently bad, but it’s
worth testing on its own to learn the true value of the
placement.
• Try Splitting: iOS / Android
***There’s an exception to every rule. There are many advertisers who do
not split placements and still see scale.***
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 60 of 93 --

8. Bidding & Budgeting
for Scale
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 61 of 93 --

Bidding for Scale
The Facebook Auction has two goals:
1. showing people ads they’ll find relevant
2. maximizing value to drive your business goals
There are three main components to bidding:
1. Facebook auction
2. Pacing system
3. Advertiser controls
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 62 of 93 --

How do you win the auction?
• YOUR BID: How much are you bidding for the
desired result?
• ESTIMATED ACTION RATE: The probability that
showing an ad to a person will deliver the desired
result.
• USER VALUE: Ad quality, post-click experience,
predicted vs. actual positive/negative feedback, as
well as ad repetition.
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 63 of 93 --

Conversion Bidding Options
After Clicking Ad
● 1 Day Click
● 7 Day Click
After Clicking or
Viewing Ad
● 1 Day Click or View
● 7 Day Click or View
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 64 of 93 --

Conversion Bidding Options
• Lowest Cost Bidding: Automatic Bidding
• Target Cost Bidding: It must be at this cost or less.
• Lowest Cost with manual bid “override”: I’m willing to pay X
per amount to get the conversion because it’s worth it to me.
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 65 of 93 --

Lowest Cost Biddingwww.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 66 of 93 --

Target Cost Biddingwww.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 67 of 93 --

Lowest Cost with Overridewww.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 68 of 93 --

Overarching Bid Strategy
• Your bid represents what you are willing to pay for the
result you want.
• Since Facebook optimizes its auction for total value, the
winner of the auction isn’t necessarily the ad with the
highest bid, but the one with the highest total value.
• This is why social proofing ads is an incredibly valuable tool,
because it gives you a higher total value score.
• Bids are also a cost-control mechanism.
• Bids can help control your desired cost per result in the
same way that budgets help control your overall spend on a
given ad set.
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 69 of 93 --

Overarching Bid Strategy
First, decide if you want to have growth and scale OR
predictable stability.
Be honest: which objective is more important to you?
Growth and scale can have three options:
• Cost-controlled scaling, which includes bigger, broader
audiences and manual bids within a CPA range (target
cost), or
• Cost-forward scaling, which includes manual bids 3-5X
the CPA goal. (“bully method”)
• Automatic bidding and letting Facebook decide.
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 70 of 93 --

Overarching Bid Strategy
Example: You have a $50 CPA goal and have had some ad sets
that have been performing well and you want to scale those but
also control costs. You should:
• Expand targeting slightly
• Turn budgets up by 10-15% per day
• Leave it on automatic bidding
If you wanted to try and scale and ensure the ads are shown more
often, forcing Facebook to show it because you feel it will convert
better, you should:
• Turn budgets up by 15% per day
• Set a manual bid of $150-300
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 71 of 93 --

Overarching Bid Strategywww.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 72 of 93 --

Overarching Bid Strategy
• In most cases for people getting started, we recommend
beginning with auto bidding, 7 day click/1 day view.
• This has the widest audience because it takes into
consideration the largest group of people within a certain
attribution window.
• However, depending on the length of your conversion
window, shorter attribution windows may be better.
• The shorter time window you select, the better short-term
wins you may see, but it will (generally speaking) be harder
to scale. For example, a 1 day click bid might do great for a
period of time but will not scale as well as a 7 day click/1
day view. (Unless a large percentage of your purchasers buy
within one day)
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 73 of 93 --

[PRO TIP] BIDDING
• You want to scale and your creative has thus far been
performing well (at least 2X ROAS or higher).
• Then try value bidding to ages 18-65 in the U.S., using your
best creatives, excluding a 180 day site visitor.
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 74 of 93 --

Bidding Scenarios
Your ads are converting, but the CPA is still too high
and you’d like to control costs.
Try a manual bid of 10% above your CPA and slowly
decrease the percentage above your CPA over a 72
hour “stabilization period” to eventually make it the
same as your CPA. OR Try target cost bidding. It might
just work!
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 75 of 93 --

Bidding Scenarios
You’re releasing a new product and you want to
ensure your ads are showing to your previous
customers or email list via the conversion objective,
since you’re confident those users will convert.
Bid 3X your CPA goal using a manual bid on a lowest
cost override.
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 76 of 93 --

Bidding Scenarios
Black Friday is approaching and you want to be sure
your ads show to the low-funnel audiences you’ve
selected.
Bid for link clicks and set a manual bid of $25. This will
ensure your ads spend very quickly and show to those
lists.
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 77 of 93 --

Budgeting
• The most common way to bid is using Daily Budgets, not
lifetime, as daily allows you to scale much easier.
• The easiest calculation is to take your total amount spent
and divide by the number of ad sets you’re running.
• Once you’ve customized the daily budget, we highly
recommend not changing anything within a 24 hour
window.
• As discussed previously, one strategy is turning ad set
budgets up and down within the same day (turning
things up as they do better during the day and down at
night). This strategy works *only* for massive accounts
spending $25k/day or more and is not recommended for
most of you.
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 78 of 93 --

9. Seasoned Pixel
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 79 of 93 --

Seasoned Pixel
• Just like a cast-iron skillet, a “seasoned pixel” will always
perform better than a brand new one.
• [PRO TIP] This is a must do! Customizing your pixel
events is absolutely essential.
• The more money you spend along with the more traffic
you have, you’re providing the Facebook pixel more
data, which in turn produces better results.
• For example, behavioral differences between a person
who purchases vs. a person who views a page.
• A seasoned pixel typically begins to happen around the
$10,000 per month level for at least two months for
Facebook to begin compiling this data.
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 80 of 93 --

www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 81 of 93 --

[PRO TIP] Stopping and Restarting
Reentering yourself into the algorithm can breathe life back
into certain ad sets. This strategy can work with concurrently
running ad sets.
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 82 of 93 --

11. Geoexpansion for Scale
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 83 of 93 --

Geoexpansion for scale
● If you can, try expanding into other countries.
● This strategy is one I’ve seen in almost all of the major
examples of scale ($500k+/mo) over the past year.
● You can still build upon the same foundations we’ve
already discussed, utilizing lookalikes and so on.
● The wider the audience in other countries, the better
off you’ll be, due to population sizes being so much
smaller.
● There are still CPAs / CPLs in the UK, Australia, and
Germany that I haven’t seen in the U.S. for 3+ years.
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 84 of 93 --

12. Reporting
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 85 of 93 --

Reporting
To make sure I’m scaling effectively and staying on track, I look at three reports every single
day:
○ Yesterday
○ Today
○ 30 Day
Each of these includes:
○ Ad Set Name
○ Amount spent
○ Results
○ Reach
○ Cost Per Result
○ Budget
○ Website Purchases
○ Website Purchase Conversion Value
○ Website Purchase ROAS
○ Link Clicks
○ CPC
○ CTR (Link)
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

13. Automated Rules
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 87 of 93 --

Automated Rules
• Automated rules are designed to make automatic
decisions based on any number of factors in relation to
how your ads are performing.
• You can easily adjust campaigns, ad sets and ads by
having Facebook automatically:
– Turning things off
– Sending a notification about performance
– Adjust budget (increase/decrease daily/lifetime
budget by X amount)
– Adjust manual bid (increase/decrease bid by X
amount)
• The options are almost endless here, and many FB
marketers have set automatic adjustments by CPA.
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 88 of 93 --
